items.title: 'Never trust a label': Mystery violin willed to youth orchestra

children.content: A violin gifted to not-for-profit organisation Music for Canberra bears the hallmarks of early 18th century European style. But one expert says only forensic testing can prove its true origins.

gallery.altText: A man with short black hair plays the violin.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short black hair plays the violin.

items.title: Rising Aussie puts world on notice with stunning record run in US

children.content: Australia's Cameron Myers puts up a new Oceania record and claims victory in a stunning mile run at the Eugene Diamond League.

image.altText: An Australian runner bursts through the finishing tape to win.

items.title: 'Pretty rare to see clearance rates this low': Property slump tipped to continue

children.content: Less than half of all properties that went to auction in Australia were actually sold over the last week.

image.altText: A house with a sold sign in the front yard.

items.title: 'Towelling the bloke up in the cells': Shocking police emails revealed

children.content: Emails reveal an extraordinary exchange between AFP officers after an Indigenous man was allegedly beaten and illegally strip-searched at a Canberra watchhouse.

gallery.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

thumbnail.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

items.title: Fears $1m to remove lead-contaminated soil in Captains Flat 'won't go very far'

children.content: Residents of Captains Flat have welcomed the promise of a $1 million fund to help them remediate lead contamination on private properties, but some fear it will not be enough.

image.altText: Signs warning of dangerous lead contamination at the road to a closed mine.

items.title: Australian in Israeli Defence Force committed war crimes, report alleges

children.content: The Australian Federal Police is considering a brief of evidence from the Australian Centre for International Justice, detailing alleged war crimes in Gaza by an Australian citizen who was in the Israeli Defence Force.

image.altText: Badge on the sleeve of Australian Federal Police officer's uniform.

Erling Haaland looks tough in celebration of a goal
Matheus Cunha holds his hands on his head while sitting on the grass

articles.title: Meet Mr Row Row, the man who invented Norway's viral World Cup Viking row

children.content: Ole Frøystad has always been a passionate football fan and when Norway qualified for its first men's World Cup since 1998, he was inspired to create the iconic chant that's become a global sensation.

image.altText: Norway fans sitting in Times Square doing a Viking row

children.content: Two goals from Erling Haaland deliver Norway an upset win over Brazil in New Jersey, before Mexico hosts England at the Azteca. Follow live.

items.title: Haaland double delivers Norway win over Brazil, England match delayed by storms

updates.title: Mexico vs England: Kick-off delayed by an hour
Three changes for England, Mexico unchanged

items.title: Shadow minister says PM's comments 'beneath his office'

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's office has released a statement, apologising for the PM's comments about Kylie Minogue on a podcast.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: As its people dig, suspicion distracts Venezuela's government

children.content: Instead of focusing all its efforts on coordinating a widescale rescue operation, the Venezuelan government has been accused of hindering the response, and as has become apparent, our ability to cover it.

image.altText: A man crying while sitting on a wall.

items.title: Albanese poised to sign major new treaty with Fiji

children.content: Anthony Albanese has arrived in Fiji to sign a major new treaty that will see the two countries "act together" against serious challenges.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese steps onto a red carpet laid out at the foot of the staircase leading to his plane.

items.title: AFP to push Australia's policing model to UN

children.content: The AFP commissioner will promote Pacific police training as a model for the United Nations when she addresses police delegates from 140 nations this week.

image.altText: Krissy Barrett

items.title: Fire damage to 10 shops after arson ram raid in Melbourne

children.content: More than 80 firefighters are at the scene in Richmond, where 10 businesses are burning after a shop was rammed and set alight this morning.

gallery.altText: Firefighters wearing helmets stand on a smoke filled street between a police car and fire engine with water coming from hoses.

thumbnail.altText: Firefighters wearing helmets stand on a smoke filled street between a police car and fire engine with water coming from hoses.

items.title: Footballer in end-of-life care after head clash in suburban game

image.altText: A football club's image of a 27-year-old on a blue background.

children.content: Epping Football Netball Club's Nathan Fitzgerald, 27, is receiving end-of-life care after colliding with a teammate in a reserves game.

items.title: Fears for imprisoned Gaza doctor Hussam Abu Safiya

children.content: Hussam Abu Safiya's lawyers believe he is at risk of being killed behind bars in an Israeli prison.

image.altText: A woman holds a sign in front of her facing that reads 'Free Dr. Hussam Abu Safiya, Free Gaza' in red, green and black text

items.title: Experts and union warn about ABC's AI use

children.content: Experts and the journalists’ union have welcomed the ABC’s plans to use artificial intelligence responsibly, but warned misuse could damage trust in the public broadcaster.

image.altText: An outdoor sign displays the ABC logo and the text "ABC" on a brown background

items.title: Is a full day of screen time for kids ever OK?

children.content: Screen time has become a phrase that makes "parents feel instantly guilty", but how detrimental is a full day of screens on the odd occasion, like during school holidays or when you are home sick?

gallery.altText: little girl sitting on ottoman watching television

thumbnail.altText: little girl sitting on ottoman watching television

items.title: These filmmakers are hunting down a story in Japan

children.content: From manga to life-sized dolls, catch up on the films from week five of Race Around the World.

image.altText: Mikaela

items.title: History quiz: Tackle our questions on Harry Potter, space stations and more

children.content: Let us test your smarts about Captain James Cook's last voyage, the debut release of a popular British girl group and other historical events.

image.altText: Harry Potter in his cloak with his wand out, in front of an eerie smoky green and black background.

items.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

articles.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration

children.content: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

image.altText: A montage of images of NAIDOC protests and image of Day of Mourning with Indigenous leaders in 1938

articles.title: As NAIDOC turns 50, what is 'deadly' and what does it mean to you?

children.content: As official NAIDOC celebrations get underway across the country, the ABC's Matt Francis asks what is deadly and what does it mean to the Indigenous community across the country.

image.altText: a man with blue aviator sunglasses smiling at the camera with a toddler wearing a black shirt with an Aboriginal flag

gallery.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

items.title: ASX set to slip despite strength in global markets

children.content: The ASX looks likely to open lower despite the Gulf cease fire holding and a positive sentiment on global markets. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: First Nations commissioner warns against Queensland adoption push

children.content: Australia's Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children commissioner has warned Queensland's push to increase adoptions could disproportionately affect First Nations children and risk repeating the harms of past forced child removals.

image.altText: Yoorrook Commissioner Sue-Anne Hunter standing against a hand rail

items.title: Boy with 'photographic memory' helping to maintain heritage sheep breed

children.content: Mitch Dickens's passion for sheep genetics saw his parents give him his own stud, which they hope will help keep a heritage breed alive.

image.altText: Boy holding up a lamb to his face and smiling.

items.title: Burns victim's family furious about Temu 'safety pledge'

children.content: Daniella Jacobs-Herd's mother is furious Temu has been approved for a government "safety pledge" while denying liability for her daughter's life-changing injuries.

image.altText: Woman with brown hair and black cardidan next to girl with brown hoodie and white t-shirt

items.title: 'Truly special': Australia wins T20 World Cup by downing England in final

children.content: Australia has won the ICC Women's T20 World Cup in commanding style, defeating England by seven wickets in the final at Lord's.

gallery.altText: Australian players celebrate Women's World Cup victory

thumbnail.altText: Australian players celebrate Women's World Cup victory

items.title: The hidden brain disease impacting DV victim-survivors

children.content: Enduring brutal physical abuse can increase the risk of developing a sinister brain disease, yet many victim-survivors are unaware, experts warn.

image.altText: Hands holding a model-brain

items.title: Woman denied bail after child found dead in NSW Central Coast home

children.content: The body of a four-year-old child has been found in a home in Wyong on the NSW Central Coast.

image.altText: a police officer stands next to a police van at the site of an alleged murder at wyong

items.title: 'Software error' blamed for F1 confusion as former driver calls for change

children.content: Despite being anti-climactic, the ending to the British Grand Prix followed the correct procedure, even if it was confusing to viewers.
